title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the US-Israeli air campaign against Iran that began with widespread strikes on February 28, 2026, special forces conducted verified ground entries in early April to rescue downed pilots, fueling trader focus on whether such operations satisfy market entry criteria. Escalation intensified over the past week as Iran rejected a US 15-point peace proposal and closed the Strait of Hormuz, prompting President Trump's April 7 threats of strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges absent a deal. With 17,000 US troops amassed nearby and Pentagon plans for limited ground operations short of invasion, upcoming large-scale airstrikes signal potential for further military action before any resolution deadline.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 16% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup following the March 31 conclusion of qualifiers and April 1 group draw, buoyed by their Euro 2024 title defense momentum and second-place FIFA ranking, just behind France who surged to No. 1 after a 2-1 friendly victory over Brazil on March 26 featuring Kylian Mbappé's goal-scoring form. The tight clustering atop the market—France (13.5%), England (11.3%), Argentina (9%), Brazil (8.6%)—mirrors a balanced 48-team field with direct qualification for CONMEBOL's top six (led by Argentina) and UEFA giants, manageable group matchups like France vs. Senegal/Norway, and resilient squads despite Brazil's Rodrygo ACL absence. Recent playoff surprises for Sweden, Czechia, and Bosnia underscore the tournament's volatility, keeping probabilities competitive across confederations.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom commands trader consensus at 24% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination in a wide-open field lacking an incumbent, propelled by his dominant March California primary poll trouncing Kamala Harris and public hints of interest at the South by Southwest conference. His executive experience as governor differentiates him from progressive firebrand Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (8%), whose media savvy appeals to the left but polarizes moderates, and Jon Ossoff (7%), a young Georgia senator buoyed by viral anti-Trump speeches amid his 2026 reelection. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results—especially battleground Senate races and gubernatorial wins—fundraising tallies, party endorsements, and the emerging primary calendar, as Democrats debate post-2024 strategy between centrism and progressivism.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid heightened US-Iran hostilities marked by ongoing US-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian energy infrastructure and Iranian missile barrages, regional mediators including Pakistan proposed a 45-day ceasefire plan on April 6—dubbed the "Islamabad Accord"—calling for an immediate truce, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and follow-on negotiations for nuclear limits in exchange for sanctions relief. Both sides rejected the terms: President Trump labeled it "not good enough" during a press conference, while Iranian officials demand a permanent end to the war rather than a temporary halt. Trump's Tuesday ultimatum looms, threatening escalated strikes on power plants and bridges if the Strait stays closed, underscoring persistent diplomatic gaps amid continued military actions.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's world No. 1 consistency—no finish outside the top 25 since the 2024 BMW Championship—and two prior green jackets anchor his trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability, but the top-heavy field remains tightly bunched amid defending champion Rory McIlroy's strong 2025 Augusta win, Jon Rahm's Hong Kong victory and top-five streak, and Bryson DeChambeau's near-miss last year. Recent Valero Texas Open champ J.J. Spaun and T2 Robert MacIntyre highlight longshot depth, while favorable dry, breezy Augusta National weather through Thursday favors low scores without favoring any stylistic edge. Practice rounds showed Scheffler and McIlroy sharpening irons, underscoring the competitive parity keeping no one above 14%.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 17.8% implied probability, just ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 16.6%, as prediction markets reflect early positioning in a fragmented field ahead of November 2026 midterms. Vance's lead stems from his incumbency advantage as Trump's VP, dominance in Republican nominee odds (38%), recent CPAC straw poll victories (53%), and a March interview dismissing Newsom as a weak opponent. Newsom tops Democratic nominee markets (24%) via national anti-Trump advocacy and SXSW hints at a post-midterm bid. The tight race persists due to untested nominees, Trump's pivotal endorsement, and low probabilities signaling uncertainty; 2026 midterm results, economic trends, scandals, or primaries could widen gaps.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 7?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior W17 car and engine advantage have propelled George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, with Russell's 43.5% implied probability slightly ahead of Antonelli's 30.8% amid their intra-team battle. Antonelli seized the championship lead with 72 points after back-to-back victories in China and Japan—his latest a strategic masterclass under safety car conditions—edging Russell by nine points despite the Briton's Australian Grand Prix win and Shanghai Sprint triumph. Russell's fourth in Japan stemmed from battery failure, fueling frustration, while Toto Wolff encourages clean intra-team racing. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trails at 49 points, highlighting Mercedes' 45-point constructors' lead as the key differentiator over rivals struggling with new regulations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder top trader consensus at 39.5% implied probability to win the 2026 NBA Championship, reflecting their grip on the Western Conference's No. 1 seed amid a dominant 56-15-ish record driven by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play and the league's best win shares. San Antonio Spurs trail at 16.3% after surging within two games of OKC, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's superstar emergence and a 16-of-17 win streak through late March that built title buzz among execs. Boston Celtics hold third at 11.8% as East powerhouses, while Detroit Pistons' recent No. 1 East clinch elevates them to 5.5% despite youth concerns; Denver Nuggets round out leaders via Nikola Jokic's double-double dominance. With nine days left, rest advantages and matchup paths shape skin-in-the-game pricing.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. leads trader consensus at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, reflecting his high-profile role championing the Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) agenda amid policy shifts on chronic disease, vaccines, and food additives that have gained traction in statehouses. Vice President J.D. Vance trails at 38% after his odds plunged to an 18% low in late March amid Trump administration's dipping approval ratings on economy, immigration, and foreign policy like Iran tensions, though partially rebounding since. Sen. Marco Rubio's 21.8% share surged recently following reports of Trump privately polling major donors who favored him over Vance, highlighting an early GOP primary divide between MAGA base loyalty and establishment backing ahead of 2026 midterms. Markets diverge from traditional polls where Vance leads.

title: Elon Musk # tweets March 31 - April 7, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have surged over 20% in the past four weeks to around $115 per barrel, driven by escalating Middle East tensions reducing shipments through the Strait of Hormuz and shutting in regional production, fostering a shift to backwardation that signals tight near-term supply. OPEC+ is implementing phased production adjustments starting April 2026 while maintaining 3.6 million b/d of voluntary cuts through year-end, countering global surpluses amid steady U.S. output near 13.3 million b/d. Despite a recent EIA-reported 5.5 million-barrel inventory build for the week ending March 27, trader consensus prices in robust demand from economic resilience. Watch upcoming EIA weekly data on April 10 and potential FOMC signals on growth for demand cues.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) at 96% implied probability for third-most seats in Colombia's Chamber of Representatives following the March 8, 2026, proportional representation election, driven by preliminary tallies showing PLC securing around 11% of votes and translating into a solid seat count behind frontrunners Centro Democrático (CD) and Partido Conservador Colombiano amid fragmented lists for coalitions like Historic Pact. Strong regional performances in departmental circunscripciones bolstered PLC's position, consistent with pre-election polling averages projecting traditional parties' resilience. Final certification by the Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil remains pending, with 16 peace curules and ethnic reserved seats allocated. Realistic challenges include recounts in tight districts, legal challenges reallocating seats, or audit adjustments shifting totals for CD or Partido de la U.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland leads Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 trader consensus at a dominant 38.8% implied probability, propelled by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's landslide UMK triumph on February 28 with "Liekinheitin"—a high-energy fusion of rock, classical violin, and dance that captivated voters nearly three-to-one and promises jury-televote balance. Recent pre-party buzz at Eurovision in Concert and OGAE poll leads (including France's 12 points) have cemented their bookmaker frontrunner status around 30%. France's Monroe ("Regarde!", internal pick March 6) and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem", DMGP winner February 14) hold steady at 10.4% each, leveraging France's direct Grand Final berth and Denmark's Nordic televote pull. Semi-final running orders set April 2 position them strongly ahead of Vienna semis May 12-14.

title: Counter-Strike: PARIVISION vs 3DMAX (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 4 match between PARIVISION and 3DMAX in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 7 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against 3DMAX.

This market will resolve to "3DMAX" if 3DMAX win the match against PARIVISION.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if PARIVISION and 3DMAX play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: PARIVISION, ranked #5 on HLTV with a 61% three-month win rate including a recent third at BLAST Open Rotterdam, face elimination pressure after a 0-2 upset loss to FUT yesterday in PGL Bucharest's Swiss group stage, dropping them to 2-2 ahead of this pivotal BO3 Round 4 high match. 3DMAX, at #15 but surging with a three-match win streak over Voca and B8 following an opening loss, leverage a lopsided 5-1 head-to-head series edge, including recent BO3 triumphs, bolstered by February roster additions like misutaaa on rifler duty. Map vetoes favor 3DMAX's Inferno pick strength, while PARIVISION's Jame-led in-game leadership and BELCHONOKK's entry fragging test tournament fatigue in the $625k event's advancement decider.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich holds a slim 40.5% trader consensus edge over Real Madrid at 37.5% for their Champions League quarterfinal first leg at the Bernabéu, with a 23.5% draw implied probability reflecting fiercely competitive dynamics. Harry Kane's confirmed availability after an ankle knock sustained internationally provides Bayern a major attacking boost, complementing their red-hot form including a 10-2 aggregate demolition of Atalanta. Real Madrid counters with home dominance and firepower from Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior, but Thibaut Courtois (thigh), Rodrygo (cruciate), and Ferland Mendy absences force Andriy Lunin into goal and test defensive depth, while Jude Bellingham's hamstring recovery limits him to a bench role. Recent squad announcements underscore the razor-thin margins in this heavyweight clash.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 98% implied probability to no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, mirroring CME FedWatch's near-unanimous pricing for holding the fed funds target at 3.50%-3.75%. This strong positioning reflects the March 17-18 FOMC's steady policy amid solid GDP growth, March nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs (unemployment at 4.3%), and February CPI inflation steady at 2.4%. Chair Powell's post-meeting remarks underscored the economy's firm footing, with the dot plot projecting just one 25 basis point cut later in 2026, likely June. Realism tempers this consensus: hotter-than-expected March CPI (due April 10) could elevate the slim 1.2% hike odds, while abrupt labor weakening might revive cut scenarios.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's commanding 65.5% implied probability as next prime minister reflects trader consensus on recent polling averages favoring his centre-right Tisza party over Viktor Orbán's incumbent Fidesz ahead of the April 12 parliamentary election. Aggregators like Politico Poll of Polls show Tisza at 49% to Fidesz's 39%, while Politpro indicates 48.7% to 40.8%; a key Medián survey from late March reported Tisza leading 58-35% among decided voters, widening from prior months and signaling strong youth support. Despite pro-Fidesz polls claiming leads of 5-23 points and Hungary's district-based electoral system advantaging incumbents, opposition surges in neutral surveys have driven odds higher, with the race tightening five days out amid voter concerns over potential interference. Minor candidates trail far behind due to fragmented support.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

markets.question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by March 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Israel airstrikes continue targeting Iranian petrochemical plants, bridges, and leadership—including the recent killing of IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi—while Iran rejects a 45-day ceasefire proposal, demanding a permanent end to hostilities and maintaining its partial closure of the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump's extended ultimatum for reopening the strait expires at 8 PM ET today, with threats of escalated attacks on energy infrastructure if unmet, fueling trader consensus on prolonged conflict amid failed mediation and tit-for-tat military actions since the war's launch on February 28. No diplomatic breakthrough has emerged, though post-deadline developments could signal de-escalation or wider escalation risks.

title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Israel in March?
markets.description:

markets.question: Will Iran strike Pakistan in March?

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ongoing 2026 Iran war, triggered by U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear, missile, and military sites starting February 28, Iran launched retaliatory ballistic missile and drone attacks against Israel and U.S. bases in the region throughout March, including multiple waves by March 31 that hit populated areas and caused damage. This escalation, amid degraded Iranian capabilities from prior U.S.-Israeli operations, resolved the market affirmatively for Iranian military action. Recent developments include Iran's April 4 cluster munition missile toward Israel and April 7 strikes damaging northern Iranian sites in response; traders now watch U.S. President Trump's April 7 press conference on potential ceasefire talks and Strait of Hormuz tensions for de-escalation signals.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from late March 2026, including Paraná Pesquisas and AtlasIntel, capture trader consensus on a razor-thin 2026 Brazilian presidential race, with incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding slim first-round leads of 41-46% over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 38-40%, while simulated runoffs show ties or narrow Flávio edges amid 6-11% undecideds. The deadlock persists due to entrenched left-right polarization, Flávio consolidating the Bolsonaro family's conservative base as other challengers like Tarcísio de Freitas trail far behind, and Lula's incumbency tempered by economic pressures and fluctuating approval ratings. Separation could arise from party conventions by July, fresh economic data, scandals, or endorsements, with the first round set for October 4 and potential runoff October 25.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes hit over 50 military targets on Kharg Island—Iran's primary Persian Gulf oil export hub handling 90% of crude shipments—in the past 24 hours, escalating tensions ahead of President Trump's Strait of Hormuz deadline without targeting energy infrastructure. Iranian state media, including Mehr News, released footage claiming the island remains in complete security under armed forces control, with oil exports uninterrupted, echoing responses to a March 13 US bombing of 90 sites. No verified loss of Iranian governmental or military control has occurred, though discussions of potential ground seizures persist; traders monitor for diplomatic breakthroughs, retaliatory actions, or further raids that could shift control dynamics.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a razor-thin lead for Candidate M at 49.5% over Iván Cepeda Castro (44.5%, Pacto Histórico left) and Paloma Valencia (40.1%, Centro Democrático center-right) in Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round vote, reflecting post-March 8 legislative elections dynamics where Cepeda clinched the left's nomination amid Pacto Histórico's congressional plurality but no majority. Recent polls (Guarumo, CNC, AtlasIntel late March) show Cepeda at 35-42% with Valencia surging from single digits to 17-22% via primary momentum and right-wing consolidation against Abelardo de la Espriella's 14-28%, keeping the field fragmented. High undecideds (over 30% in surveys) and Petro's mixed approval sustain tightness; separation could stem from April debates, endorsements, economic data, or scandals ahead of potential June 21 runoff.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Sporting CP (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Sporting CP" if Sporting CP win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Spread: Arsenal FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP face Arsenal in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at Estádio José Alvalade, with the hosts severely hampered by key absences: winger Nuno Santos sidelined by a thigh injury for around three weeks and captain Morten Hjulmand suspended, eroding defensive width and midfield control against Arsenal's high-pressing style. Both could return for the April 15 second leg at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal navigate their own injury concerns post-Carabao Cup final loss to Manchester City, with Mikel Merino out (foot), Eberechi Eze doubtful (month sidelined), and Martin Ødegaard, Jurrien Timber, Leandro Trossard as question marks, though no fresh issues reported recently. Arsenal boast nine UCL wins from ten this season, while Sporting reach their first quarter-final amid strong Primeira Liga form but recent fixture rescheduling for extra preparation. Home advantage and aggregate scoring favor a cautious Sporting approach, but trader consensus likely reflects Arsenal's edge in squad depth and momentum.

title: Counter-Strike: FOKUS vs BC.Game Esports (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 4 match between FOKUS and BC.Game Esports in the PGL Bucharest Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 7 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FOKUS" if FOKUS win the match against BC.Game Esports.

This market will resolve to "BC.Game Esports" if BC.Game Esports win the match against FOKUS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FOKUS (#36 HLTV), a newly assembled international squad coached by kuben with rifler volt and AWPer Jorko shining, entered PGL Bucharest 2026 Swiss group stage round 4 at 1-2 after upsetting 3DMAX (2-1) but dropping to PARIVISION and Wildcard, leveraging strong Ancient (92% win rate) and Overpass (67%) preferences in the map veto. BC.Game (#46), featuring stars s1mple and electroNic under coach TaZ, sat at 1-2 following a win over Voca but heavy losses to The MongolZ and MIBR, hampered by poor Ancient form (13% wins) and roster tweaks like benching jkaem. This do-or-die BO3 highlighted FOKUS's cohesion from open qualifier run against BC.Game's underperforming superteam, shaping trader focus on momentum over pedigree.

If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eighth seed Alexander Bublik enters the Rolex Monte-Carlo Masters round of 32 as trader consensus favorite over veteran Gael Monfils on clay, reflecting his No. 11 ATP ranking, potent serve, and fresher legs after a first-round bye, contrasting Monfils' gritty three-set opener win over Tallon Griekspoor two days ago. Monfils holds a 2-1 head-to-head edge—their last meeting a 2023 Washington hard-court victory—but this marks his farewell appearance at the event amid a 2026 retirement tour. Bublik eyes a repeat of his strong 2025 clay results, while Monfils' baseline movement and experience could fuel an upset on the slow Monte Carlo courts; both players report fit with no injury issues.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 95.8% for the Iranian regime falling by April 30, reflecting the Islamic Republic's demonstrated resilience amid ongoing US-Israeli airstrikes under Operation Epic Fury. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination on February 28 prompted a swift Assembly of Experts election of his son Mojtaba as successor by early March, stabilizing the leadership vacuum without sparking mass uprisings or military defections. Despite infrastructure hits—bridges, energy facilities, petrochemical plants—and crackdowns quelling protests, IRGC forces maintain territorial control, with intelligence assessments confirming no imminent collapse. President Trump's recent ultimatums demanding Strait of Hormuz reopening carry escalation risks, but absent a full invasion, popular revolt, or economic meltdown, the hydra-like structure endures.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's formal nomination of former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh as next Fed Chair, submitted to the Senate on March 4, has propelled trader consensus to 96% implied probability of confirmation before Jerome Powell's term ends May 15. Recent scheduling of a Senate Banking Committee hearing on April 16 signals advancing momentum through the confirmation process, bolstered by Warsh's experience navigating the 2008 financial crisis and Republican Senate majority control. Despite GOP Sen. Thom Tillis's prior holdout over a DOJ probe into Powell, the proceeding date suggests resolution or workaround. Potential challenges include committee delays, floor vote opposition, or prolonged legal issues extending past the deadline.
